name: George Gershwin
abstract: George Gershwin (; born Jacob Gershwine; September 26, 1898 – July 11, 1937) was an American composer and pianist whose compositions spanned jazz, popular and classical music. Among his best-known works are the songs "Swanee" (1919) and "Fascinating Rhythm" (1924), the orchestral compositions Rhapsody in Blue (1924) and An American in Pa…

about_quotes.speaker: Ted Piltzecker
about_quotes.evidence: George needed a vibraphone player for a quintet a year or two later... I said, “Great, sure, why not... managed to play well enough to get the gig. So like the next week I’m in Los Angeles, you know, playing with the quintet
about_quotes.doc_title: Ted Piltzecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 10, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: George Gershwin gets a little more, you know, Harold Arlen, it gets a little more sophisticated and you’ve got to learn all those other little side turns.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: If George Gershwin were alive and he could sue you for stealing chord changes, I would be in jail just like Dizzy Gillespie would be in jail.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: in that Golden Age you heard the best American music, all the Gershwin, Kern, Duke Ellington, Hoagy Carmichael stuff, all over the world.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Barrett
about_quotes.evidence: go out and buy George Gershwin songs you know. And look at them and look at the piano parts... these are great composers you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Barrett interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: He said, “in the 1930s I was there with George Gershwin two rows over... They were studying this way of making their music fresh”
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007
